Alright, you guys all having a great time so far? Awesome. Okay, I am very excited for our next speaker For a couple reasons the first time I learned about this guy was probably about six months ago I got an email from a guy named Mike Filson You probably heard about him and he sent an email he said hey There's this guy and he spends over he spent over a hundred million dollars in paid media And if you want to learn how to drive traffic like no one else in the world can do you need to buy this guy's course So I went to the website I read the sales letter and it wasn't the greatest sales letter in the world no offense But the like the credibility and stuff. Yeah, the first internet wave was hitting man like I moved back from LA and I walked into this incredible world like Companies like Netscape were out there and so my first job was actually selling data center services So I sold like co-location Services to folks like Netscape to Apple to excited home like those eBay and really, you know I I really found this world that was like didn't have any rules and like I remember my first job I rolled in with a suit and tie to a company called wired wired magazine and they actually had a wall okay of ties that were cut off and like pounded to the wall and You know so I'm like this is this is really I was classically trained sales guy But but I saw all this stuff and the guy said look do you want me to take it off now? Do you want to take it off or should I cut it off? So it really introduced me to this really radical world that was happening in Silicon Valley in 1996 I sold the companies like Netscape go to Lycos excite Yahoo I became really good friends with the founder of eBay right talk about really blowing your mind type Experience when you hang out with these guys So I saw like this massive massive amount of wealth being created that I'd never seen before in my life I had worked as an employee for many many years Struggling at struggling trying to make it work when I saw this massive wealth creation. It just absolutely inspired me I saw that you could actually make a ton of money doing this creating great companies. That's what I did You know, I it's like the second law of overnight success. You just work your F&S off I mean I was out there pounding the streets like when I when you see this kind of money being created It creates massive massive amount of motivation for you So I did that I created a bunch of companies online First company was a company called metal reward. It was one of the earliest affiliate networks We started this in 1999 and I mentioned 99 because you know what happened in 99, right? Like all this stuff crashed like the entire thing crashed It's like what we're going through now a massive recession, right in 99 the entire bubble crashed So there was just like death and destruction everywhere in Silicon Valley all the guys that had moved there to make money They all went home So I point that out because in my experience the best time to start businesses are actually right now Like the amount of wealth and talent and people out there that you could hire for almost next to nothing is extraordinary so metal reward we Had an idea. We had some angel investors. We sat at the table just like this We've not a nickel to our name and we just busted our ass like we worked super super hard We created an affiliate network affiliate network simple job is to it's to take advertisers and put them together with publishers We sold that business for 30 million bucks to a company called Expedia so we sold that business for 30 million bucks, which is pretty cool back in 2002 Turns out the venture guys make most of the money Which is one thing you might realize down the road if you ever sell a company that's venture back But nonetheless we sold it for 30 million bucks My biggest partner and I we peeled away We created a company called Konexus and Konexus was a lead generation company We were one of the first guys to use incentivized marketing back in the day We basically grew it again plastic desk from IKEA from two employees to over 130 employees We took it from doing zero to doing 130 million dollars a year in three years. Okay, that's monster growth guys That's like incredible growth. We raised 20 million dollars in venture capital. Okay, so it turns out the customers have two components, right? All right, so it's two things traffic and conversion and again, I study this stuff fairly regularly and and Trying to put myself in the perspective of people learning traffic generation There's always been one of my goals, but but has anyone ever lost money media buying? Like a lot anyone lose like a thousand bucks or ten thousand bucks. Yeah, it's hard right? It's like it's it's just confute media buying can be very confusing And so I I put together a slide which I think tries to address why that is First off The way that media buying tends to be taught now is not fundamental based Like I teach people fundamentals of media buying first then tactics later I'm gonna share with you a tactic who wants to know like for example how to get 300,000 fan fan pages in like 30 days Like and this guy actually built a list of 60,000 opt-ins off of Facebook for free. Yeah pretty good like You know like or I can show you how to get 40 to 60,000 visitors to your to your website every day Yeah, pretty like I can teach you this stuff There's there's no shortage of tactics that are out there But where most people fail is they don't take the time to learn the fundamentals, okay? And so I'm really a fundamental based teacher which makes me somewhat boring because like my landing pages They suck because they don't say like push this magic button and get ten thousand bucks Like that's where my marketing sucks like I don't I'm not a hypey. I teach like get rich over time I don't necessarily teach like get rich quick. So fundamentals and this stupid thing. Let's see. This is from Google, right? I think Google I Think Google is trying to sabotage me right now My god bastard Google What if I put this like right down there? Wow, see what I mean. It's like powerful stuff. No, I'm just kidding. I'm just kidding. I'm not a conspiracy theorist Anyway fundamentals are very rarely taught in this business There are a lot of bad teachers out there a lot of bad teachers, okay? I think this topic gets a lot of hype people like media buying but a lot of quite simply bad teachers that teach this stuff and You know, I know Russell's a wrestler like I've been doing jujitsu for like 12 years And I can say this stuff because I just I welcome these bad guys It like challenged me physically because it'd be great right I can take them down But a lot of really bad teachers out there So the reason that Russell's here the rest there's a there's a cadre of guys that I look after That are teaching this stuff the right way. There's an army of guys a lot of them on click bank that the teach bad stuff And I just for that I feel I feel bad as a teacher of this stuff And finally there's information overload like there's a lot hitting all of us each day, right? There's banner retargeting network. There's Facebook So part of the challenge in making people great at media buying is getting them to focus on one single channel And that's what I'll try to help you guys do today. Okay? the other part of You know media buying is conversion, right? Conversion is hard like you can get visitors all day long I guarantee it I can drive a hundred thousand people to your landing page probably tomorrow If I make a few phone calls, I know I can the problem is conversion people know how to convert customers So there's all kinds of issues relating to take you take them to a video landing page a long-form sales an email opt-in So you have to study conversion as a second part of your equation So when we work with guys like Frank Kern these guys get conversion like he's a good marketer, okay? He understands the whole game guys like Mike Phil same Jeff Johnson These guys are really good at converting where they struggle a bit is media buying because they exist in kind of this joint venture World and I'll tell you the world outside of joint venture marketing is about a billion times bigger than the joint venture world That's why people are breaking out of that mold and going to media buying which is outside of their world, okay? So conversion can be tricky, but I can teach you how to convert, okay? I want to say that you guys can do this like I believe this in my heart like who me how many people believe you can do this Anybody yeah, you can do this. Okay. I've done it five times I know you can do it as well, and I'll tell you there's an army of guys who have done it before you I call them traffic bandits These are guys that have collectively over the years stolen traffic from the biggest brands out there, okay? I being one of them my seminal moment was receiving a call from Steven bomber at three in the morning Do you guys know who Steven bomber is? Yeah, he's the CEO of Microsoft and that's pretty badass like see of Microsoft So I was in the front page of MSN comm and I had a really annoying banner, and if you guys have seen it I'm sorry in advance like I had a Banner because I was obsessed with click-through rate. And it's usually sold in an auction type format So the best ad goes to the very very top and guys this is massive massive volume Me who who in this room wants to advertise in the front page of like MSMBC or Fox or ESPN or USA today? Okay, these are the things that when I was doing it would cost me 10 grand 20 grand now You can do it for as little as a hundred bucks So the power of ad networks allows you to get onto some super targeted pages for very very little amount of money and very very Little around a little amount of risk so again places like this places That would normally cost me an arm and a leg that the reason why ad networks are so great is because they can allow they've taken Google's model of a cost per click and self service and They have moved it into big big placements like this So you can routinely get in the front page of these big big sites For almost no money, okay? You got to learn the basics again. I mean there's a lot of elements that go into this stuff So my material is a lot of material on learning the basics if I can teach you the basics of this stuff I think it gets remarkably easier from there get great at one thing now Here's where people fail in media buying they get so excited about it So they open up an account on Facebook Twitter they go traffic vans to do all these things and they're okay at everything If I can give you one piece of advice with media buying start somewhere and stay there Okay, if you start with Facebook don't go anywhere else if I catch you going anywhere else I'll hit your hand or something, but just stick with one area The problem is people get enamored with all the shiny lights out there of oh banner retargeting or or or you know Twitter marketing or Facebook marketing I mean just stop and just do one thing great all the good guys I know all of all of them without exception started one place and the great guys expanded out from There but if you start with kind of this like scattershot approach You will probably lose because you're competing with guys that are great. You got to take action. You got to put your money down I'm encouraged that people have bought so much Facebook advertising you got to keep doing it You got to build that muscle up you can't expect to make money immediately. It's it's a process. So take action and then finally Have a little patience. Okay. Have a little patience. This stuff does take some time. All right Let's get to the actual formula that I have used over the last decade to create five seven eight nine figure businesses and guys I don't want you to Be like washed up in the simplicity of it. It's a fairly simple formula But with the biggest guys I know They all follow this formula. So if you can follow it, I think you'll be much more successful One I said choose a traffic source. Okay. There's a lot to that, right? If you have a particular niche you got to figure out where your buyers are hanging out They're probably hanging out on Facebook to be honest with you at some level. So pick one traffic source I said this earlier Use tools and I'll go over this a little bit more detail in a second But use tools to study the winners the ads that you see each every day online are making somebody money, right? Most of the dumb money is washed out of the system So the people the ads that you see repeatedly like I go to places like, you know drudge report or ESPN or whatever I have a different eye of looking at ads, but the ads I'm seeing repeatedly are making somebody money. So study the winners, okay? Reverse engineer. It's a very fancy word for saying copy. Okay, again I can say this because Russell is a good wrestler who backed me up Copy people copy the winners a cop copy the ones that have already invested the time and energy to making their campaign work Okay, now, you know, there's don't copy everything But the point is you've got to be able to study the winners and copy them, okay? Then test go out and try to do your own tests record and optimize those So this is a big part of bass is record all your data along the way And if you can do that what happens with your data and I use Excel Microsoft now it's Google Docs, which actually they both not cool But but if you look at my math, maybe I could show you guys like It's very formulaic like I can see dips and conversion rates based on every time I change a piece of creative and I'll test like for example when you're testing at high volume 90% of your inventory goes to your winners 10% goes to your testing. Okay, there's things like that There's ad servers involved. So there's all elements that come into play here, but more than anything It's like if I can get you all making a hundred bucks a day 500 bucks a day thousand bucks a day That's always my goal people get kind of caught up in all the technology you need to get going You don't you know you need to excel a cell phone a computer and some ingenuity to make this stuff happen. Okay And then finally the biggest guys the best guys scaled and they won and there's all kinds of elements relative to scaling You should be considerate considerate about Cash flows being one of them Your infrastructure is another one if it's a shared server or dedicated There's all kinds of intricacies that if we had 10 hours, I could share with you that would be relative to Scaling your infrastructure. All right, so again step one please please please choose one traffic source I recommend Facebook if you want to get more advanced go to an ad network if you have some money to spend go to a big portal Okay, this is the strategy that we use for peak performance golf these guys were sitting at zero We took ten thousand bucks a month and we blew them out on MSN now. I highlight two one free one paid Okay, Quantcast you guys all use Quantcast Use Quantcast Quantcast will help you identify your target market. They'll help you set up your profiles on Facebook One right one technique that I use is I have multiple profiles on Facebook as a user. So if I'm selling to for example a 35 year old woman I become a 35 year old and we do that a lot in San Francisco That's like totally different topic totally different topic But no, I mean I become a 35 year old woman on Facebook because I want to understand the types of ads that she sees every day Right, okay, so that's a technique that you should study But nonetheless places like Quantcast will help you a lot in terms of figuring out like who is the 900 pound gorilla in your space How are they doing it? They're places like moat dot com which some friends of mine that started to write media Started okay, that's a free search which will tell you the creative that's running there. There are services like adbeat You guys know adbeat? Adbeat's an amazing service what adbeat does is it goes out and it surveys 5,000 sites Google included 5,000 sites it pulls down all the creative it sees it shows you the creative the landing page the affiliate link It shows you and an ordinate amount of data So the point here is you have to be willing to use data if you're gonna win at this space ad beats written by a remarkable Marketer that was doing incredible numbers online So I trust his stuff but ad beats a great service And if you want to figure out like who's in your niche and how are they doing it? You can type in their domain name and it'll point out to you exactly where they're buying media today So it'll tell you a lot of the story right pretty powerful stuff, right?
